articles.title: Rajasthan Weather: Severe Storm Alert for 38 Districts on April 7 with Hail and Strong Winds - patrika.com
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Rajasthan Weather Update: A significant change is expected in the weather of Rajasthan once again. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has declared the upcoming April 7 as an extremely 'dangerous' day for the state.
According to the latest bulletin from the Meteorological Department, a 'Yellow Alert' has been issued for 38 out of the state's 41 districts. During this period, strong winds (dust storms) will blow at speeds of 40 to 50 kilometres per hour, with a strong possibility of thunderstorms and lightning.
Today, April 4, the weather will remain adverse across the entire state, except for Phalodi, Jodhpur, Jaisalmer, Bikaner, Barmer, and Balotra. The IMD has issued Yellow and Orange Alerts for most parts of the state. Today, there are chances of dust storms blowing at a speed of 40 to 50 km per hour throughout the day, accompanied by thunderstorms and rain.
The good news is that there is no major warning for any district of the state on April 5. However, the weather will change again from April 6. On Saturday, a Yellow Alert will be in effect for Kotputli-Behror, Sikar, Bikaner, Churu, Didwana-Kuchaman, Hanumangarh, and Jhunjhunu districts.
April 7 is considered the most challenging day for Rajasthan. The districts for which alerts have been issued include Jaipur, Ajmer, Alwar, Beawar, Bharatpur, Bhilwara, Bundi, Chittorgarh, Dausa, Deeg, Dholpur, Jhalawar, Jhunjhunu, Karauli, Khairthal-Tijara, Kota, Kotputli-Behror, Pratapgarh, Rajsamand, Salumbar, Sawai Madhopur, Sikar, Sirohi, Tonk, Udaipur, Balotra, Barmer, Bikaner, Kuchaman, Hanumangarh, Jaisalmer, Jalore, Jodhpur, Nagaur, Pali, Phalodi, and Ganganagar.
It is noteworthy that a severe dust storm on April 3 caused heavy devastation in the state, claiming the lives of two people. Heavy hailstorms occurred in many northern districts, including Ganganagar and Hanumangarh, causing significant damage to crops as well. The administration has advised the public to stay away from trees and electricity poles during adverse weather conditions and seek shelter in safe places.
Published on:
04 Apr 2026 10:01 am

articles.title: Storm and rain to continue in Rajasthan, alert issued: Hail warning issued, two deaths in Jaipur, crops hit... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Storm and rain to continue in Rajasthan, alert issued:Hail warning issued, two deaths in Jaipur, crops hit in fields and mandis
The impact of a western disturbance will continue in Rajasthan on April 4. The Meteorological Centre in Jaipur has issued an alert for heavy rain in more than 25 districts today as well. On Friday (April 3), heavy rainfall was recorded in over 10 districts.
Hailstorms were also reported in Bikaner, Sri Ganganagar and Jaisalmer. Meanwhile, in Jaipur city, two people died in separate incidents due to a dust storm that hit late in the evening.
Due to this change in weather, daytime temperatures have dropped by up to 7 degrees Celsius. Rain has also caused damage to crops in mandis and fields.
The spell of dust storms and rain will not stop yet
According to Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Meteorological Centre Jaipur, due to the current system, strong winds, rain and hail are likely at some places on April 4 in parts of Udaipur, Ajmer, Kota, Jaipur, Bharatpur divisions and the Shekhawati region.
A slight decrease in storm and rain activity is expected on April 5 and 6. However, another strong system is likely to become active on April 7, bringing fresh chances of rain and storms.
2 deaths in accidents due to storm
In Jaipur, the storm and rain that began around 5 pm on Friday continued till about 11 pm. The strong winds caused significant damage in several parts of the city.
In Jawahar Nagar area, a man riding a motorcycle died on the spot after an electric pole fell on him. The deceased was identified as 50-year-old Ramjilal Mahawar, a resident of Mukesh Nagar.
Ramjilal worked in a field related to electricity poles. Another incident occurred in the Jawahar Circle police station area, where a granite stone fell on an elderly man due to strong winds, leading to his death on the spot.
Rabi crops damaged due to rain and storm
Usually, western disturbances begin to weaken after March, but this time systems have remained active continuously since March 13. By the end of March, five systems had developed, and by the first week of April, six systems have already become active.
According to meteorologists, such frequent formation of systems in a short span is unusual and has altered the weather pattern, directly impacting agriculture.
In several districts of Rajasthan, mustard, wheat and gram crops have suffered the most damage. Crops kept in the open in mandis have also been soaked.
Hailstorm in the first week of April for the second time in 5 years
Storm, rain and hailstorm occurred in these districts
In the last 24 hours, rainfall was recorded in Bikaner, Sri Ganganagar, Hanumangarh, Churu, Jaisalmer, Jhunjhunu, Sikar, Nagaur, Barmer, Jodhpur, Jalore, Jaipur, Ajmer, Alwar, Tonk and other districts.
Rainfall recorded included 17.6 mm in Ajmer, 11.5 mm in Hanumangarh, 12.2 mm in Jhunjhunu, 4 mm in Nagaur, 1.4 mm in Ganganagar, 1.5 mm in Jodhpur, 3 mm in Sikar and 1.9 mm in Pilani.
Temperature dropped by 7 degrees, mild cold during the day
Following the storm, rain and hail, most cities in the state recorded a drop in temperature. Maximum temperatures fell by 7 to 8 degrees Celsius in several cities, including Sri Ganganagar.
A mild chill was felt during the day on Friday in these cities. The highest maximum temperature on Friday was recorded at 35.6 degrees Celsius in Chittorgarh and Alwar.
